food_pairing.name: Condrieu with quenelle de brochet
food_pairing.pairing: Condrieu Viognier with quenelle de brochet sauce Nantua
food_pairing.description: The Lyonnais pike dumpling in its classic crayfish-cream sauce finds a natural partner in Condrieu. The wine's apricot, honey and floral richness mirrors the sweetness of crayfish while its weight handles the cream sauce without being overwhelmed.

food_pairing.name: Cornas with andouillette lyonnaise
food_pairing.pairing: Cornas Syrah with andouillette a la lyonnaise
food_pairing.description: The pungent, minerally andouillette sausage of Lyon demands a wine of equal intensity. Cornas Syrah on granite brings iron, smoked meat and dark fruit that hold their own against the sausage's assertive charcuterie character.

food_pairing.name: Hermitage rouge with tablier de sapeur
food_pairing.pairing: Hermitage or Saint-Joseph rouge with tablier de sapeur
food_pairing.description: The breaded and pan-fried tripe preparation of Lyon needs a structured Northern Rhone red. Hermitage and Saint-Joseph Syrah bring firm tannins and savoury iron mineral that cut the richness of the offal without overwhelming its delicate flavour.

food_pairing.name: Tavel rose with saucisson de Lyon
food_pairing.pairing: Tavel AOC rose with saucisson de Lyon and charcuterie
food_pairing.description: The structured, full-bodied dry rose of Tavel is the ideal companion for a Lyonnais charcuterie spread anchored by saucisson. The wine's garrigue freshness, mineral backbone and red-fruit brightness balance the salt and fat of coarse pork sausage.

food_pairing.name: Cote-Rotie with salade lyonnaise
food_pairing.description: The warm frisee, lardons, croutons and poached egg of the salade lyonnaise call for a supple, perfumed red. A village-level or approachable Cote-Rotie brings violet, white pepper and red fruit that flatter the bacon without competing with the egg.

food_pairing.name: Chateauneuf-du-Pape rouge with pate en croute lyonnais
food_pairing.pairing: Chateauneuf-du-Pape rouge with pate en croute lyonnais
food_pairing.description: The pastry-wrapped terrine of Lyon, stuffed with pistachio and foie gras, meets a natural partner in a Southern Rhone red. Chateauneuf-du-Pape's garrigue warmth, dark fruit and generous body complement the richness of the filling without over-powering the delicate crust.

food_pairing.name: Condrieu or Saint-Peray blanc with cervelle de canut
food_pairing.pairing: Northern Rhone white (Condrieu or Marsanne) with cervelle de canut
food_pairing.description: The herbed, garlicky fromage blanc of Lyon is a classic muse venue plate. A rich, aromatic Northern Rhone white brings floral peach or beeswax texture that harmonises with the creamy cheese while its mineral finish cuts the cream.

food_pairing.name: Rasteau VDN with tarte aux pralines
food_pairing.pairing: Rasteau Vin Doux Naturel with tarte aux pralines
food_pairing.description: The bright-pink praline tart of Lyon, rich with caramelised sugar and almond cream, finds a harmonious counterpart in the sweet, oxidative Rasteau VDN. The wine's fig, coffee and dried cherry notes echo the nutty-caramel sweetness of the pralines.
